title: CopilotApi
description: Describes all JSON-RPC methods exposed by the Copilot CLI server. SDK codegen tools consume this to produce typed client wrappers.

ping.description: Checks server responsiveness and returns protocol information.

params.description: Optional message to echo back to the caller.

result.description: Server liveness response, including the echoed message, current server timestamp, and protocol version.

connect.description: Performs the SDK server connection handshake and validates the optional connection token.

params.description: Optional connection token presented by the SDK client during the handshake.

result.description: Handshake result reporting the server's protocol version and package version on success.

list.description: Lists Copilot models available to the authenticated user.

params.description: Optional GitHub token used to list models for a specific user instead of the global auth context.

result.description: List of Copilot models available to the resolved user, including capabilities and billing metadata.

list.description: Lists built-in tools available for a model.

params.description: Optional model identifier whose tool overrides should be applied to the listing.

result.description: Built-in tools available for the requested model, with their parameters and instructions.

getQuota.description: Gets Copilot quota usage for the authenticated user or supplied GitHub token.

params.description: Optional GitHub token used to look up quota for a specific user instead of the global auth context.

result.description: Quota usage snapshots for the resolved user, keyed by quota type.

addFilterValues.description: Registers secret values for redaction in session logs and exports. The SDK calls this to inject dynamically generated secret values (e.g., OIDC tokens).

params.description: Secret values to add to the redaction filter.

result.description: Confirmation that the secret values were registered.

list.description: Lists MCP servers from user configuration.

result.description: User-configured MCP servers, keyed by server name.

add.description: Adds an MCP server to user configuration.

params.description: MCP server name and configuration to add to user configuration.

update.description: Updates an MCP server in user configuration.

params.description: MCP server name and replacement configuration to write to user configuration.

remove.description: Removes an MCP server from user configuration.

params.description: MCP server name to remove from user configuration.

enable.description: Enables MCP servers in user configuration for new sessions.

params.description: MCP server names to enable for new sessions.

disable.description: Disables MCP servers in user configuration for new sessions.

params.description: MCP server names to disable for new sessions.

reload.description: Drops this runtime process's in-memory MCP server-definition cache so the next MCP config read observes disk.

discover.description: Discovers MCP servers from user, workspace, plugin, and builtin sources.

params.description: Optional working directory used as context for MCP server discovery.

result.description: MCP servers discovered from user, workspace, plugin, and built-in sources.

setDisabledSkills.description: Replaces the global list of disabled skills.

params.description: Skill names to mark as disabled in global configuration, replacing any previous list.

discover.description: Discovers skills across global and project sources.

params.description: Optional project paths and additional skill directories to include in discovery.

result.description: Skills discovered across global and project sources.

reload.description: Drops this runtime process's in-memory user settings cache so the next settings read observes disk.

shutdown.description: Gracefully shuts down an SDK-owned runtime. The response is sent only after cleanup completes; callers may then terminate the owned runtime process.

setProvider.description: Registers an SDK client as the session filesystem provider.

params.description: Initial working directory, session-state path layout, and path conventions used to register the calling SDK client as the session filesystem provider.

result.description: Indicates whether the calling client was registered as the session filesystem provider.

fork.description: Creates a new session by forking persisted history from an existing session.

params.description: Source session identifier to fork from, optional event-ID boundary, and optional friendly name for the new session.

result.description: Identifier and optional friendly name assigned to the newly forked session.

connect.description: Connects to an existing remote session and exposes it as an SDK session.

params.description: Remote session connection parameters.

result.description: Remote session connection result.

list.description: Lists persisted sessions, optionally filtered by working-directory context.

params.description: Optional metadata-load limit and filters applied to the returned sessions.

result.description: Persisted sessions matching the filter, ordered most-recently-modified first.

findByTaskId.description: Finds the local session bound to a GitHub task ID, if any.

params.description: GitHub task ID to look up.

result.description: ID of the local session bound to the given GitHub task, or omitted when none.

findByPrefix.description: Resolves a UUID prefix to a unique session ID, if exactly one session matches.

params.description: UUID prefix to resolve to a unique session ID.

result.description: Session ID matching the prefix, omitted when no unique match exists.

getLastForContext.description: Returns the most-relevant prior session for a given working-directory context.

params.description: Optional working-directory context used to score session relevance.

result.description: Most-relevant session ID for the supplied context, or omitted when no sessions exist.

getEventFilePath.description: Computes the absolute path to a session's persisted events.jsonl file.

params.description: Session ID whose event-log file path to compute.

result.description: Absolute path to the session's events.jsonl file on disk.

getSizes.description: Returns the on-disk byte size of each session's workspace directory.

result.description: Map of sessionId -> on-disk size in bytes for each session's workspace directory.

checkInUse.description: Returns the subset of the supplied session IDs that are currently held by another running process.

params.description: Session IDs to test for live in-use locks.

result.description: Session IDs from the input set that are currently in use by another process.

getPersistedRemoteSteerable.description: Returns a session's persisted remote-steerable flag, if any has been recorded.

params.description: Session ID to look up the persisted remote-steerable flag for.

result.description: The session's persisted remote-steerable flag, or omitted when no value has been persisted.

close.description: Closes a session: emits shutdown, flushes pending events, releases the in-use lock, and disposes the active session.

params.description: Session ID to close.

result.description: Closes a session: emits shutdown, flushes pending events to disk, releases the in-use lock, disposes the active session. Idempotent: succeeds even if the session is not currently active.

bulkDelete.description: Closes, deactivates, and deletes a set of sessions, returning the bytes freed per session.

params.description: Session IDs to close, deactivate, and delete from disk.

result.description: Map of sessionId -> bytes freed by removing the session's workspace directory.

pruneOld.description: Deletes sessions older than the given threshold, with optional dry-run and exclusion list.

params.description: Age threshold and optional flags controlling which old sessions are pruned (or simulated when dryRun is true).

result.description: Outcome of the prune operation: deleted IDs, dry-run candidates, skipped IDs, total bytes freed, and the dry-run flag.

save.description: Flushes a session's pending events to disk.

params.description: Session ID whose pending events should be flushed to disk.

result.description: Flush a session's pending events to disk. No-op when no writer exists for the session (e.g., already closed).

releaseLock.description: Releases the in-use lock held by this process for a session.

params.description: Session ID whose in-use lock should be released.

result.description: Release the in-use lock held by this process for the given session. No-op when this process does not currently hold a lock for the session.

enrichMetadata.description: Backfills missing summary and context fields on the supplied session metadata records.

params.description: Session metadata records to enrich with summary and context information.

result.description: The enriched metadata records, with summary and context fields backfilled where available. Sessions confirmed empty and unnamed are omitted.

reloadPluginHooks.description: Reloads user, plugin, and (optionally) repo hooks on the active session.

params.description: Active session ID and an optional flag for deferring repo-level hooks until folder trust.

result.description: Reload all hooks (user, plugin, optionally repo) and apply them to the active session. Call after installing or removing plugins so their hooks take effect immediately. No-op when no active session matches the given sessionId.

loadDeferredRepoHooks.description: Loads previously-deferred repo-level hooks on the active session, returning queued startup prompts.

params.description: Active session ID whose deferred repo-level hooks should be loaded.

result.description: Queued repo-level startup prompts and the total hook command count after loading.

setAdditionalPlugins.description: Replaces the manager-wide additional plugins registered with the session manager.

params.description: Manager-wide additional plugins to register; replaces any previously-configured set.

result.description: Replace the manager-wide additional plugins. New session creations and subsequent hook reloads see the new set; already-running sessions keep their existing hook installation until the next reload.

spawn.description: Spawns a managed-server child with the supplied configuration and returns a discriminated-union result. The caller (typically the CLI controller) is responsible for attaching to the spawned child and sending any follow-up prompt. When the controller-local spawn gate is closed the server returns JSON-RPC MethodNotFound.

params.description: Inputs to spawn a managed-server child via the controller's spawn delegate.

result.description: Outcome of an agentRegistry.spawn call.

suspend.description: Suspends the session while preserving persisted state for later resume.

params.description: Identifies the target session.

send.description: Sends a user message to the session and returns its message ID.

displayPrompt.description: If provided, this is shown in the timeline instead of `prompt`

items.description: A user message attachment — a file, directory, code selection, blob, GitHub reference, or extension-supplied context payload

attachments.description: Optional attachments (files, directories, selections, blobs, GitHub references) to include with the message

mode.description: How to deliver the message. `enqueue` (default) appends to the message queue. `immediate` interjects during an in-progress turn.

prepend.description: If true, adds the message to the front of the queue instead of the end

billable.description: If false, this message will not trigger a Premium Request Unit charge. User messages default to billable.

requiredTool.description: If set, the request will fail if the named tool is not available when this message is among the user messages at the start of the current exchange

source.description: Optional provenance tag copied to the resulting user.message event. Supported values are `system`, `command-*`, and `schedule-*`.

agentMode.description: The UI mode the agent was in when this message was sent. Defaults to the session's current mode.

requestHeaders.description: Custom HTTP headers to include in outbound model requests for this turn. Merged with session-level provider headers; per-turn headers augment and overwrite session-level headers with the same key.

traceparent.description: W3C Trace Context traceparent header for distributed tracing of this agent turn

tracestate.description: W3C Trace Context tracestate header for distributed tracing

wait.description: If true, await completion of the agentic loop for this message before returning. Defaults to false (fire-and-forget). When true, the result still contains the same `messageId`; the caller can rely on the agent having processed the message before the call resolves.

params.description: Parameters for sending a user message to the session
params.title: SendRequest

result.description: Result of sending a user message

reason.description: Finite reason code describing why the current turn was aborted

params.description: Parameters for aborting the current turn
params.title: AbortRequest

result.description: Result of aborting the current turn

shutdown.description: Shuts down the session and persists its final state. Awaits any deferred sessionEnd hooks before resolving so user-supplied hook scripts complete before the runtime tears down.

type.description: Why the session is being shut down. Defaults to "routine" when omitted.

reason.description: Optional human-readable reason. Typically the message of the error that triggered shutdown when type is 'error'.

params.description: Parameters for shutting down the session
params.title: ShutdownRequest

getStatus.description: Gets authentication status and account metadata for the session.

params.description: Identifies the target session.

result.description: Authentication status and account metadata for the session.

setCredentials.description: Updates the session's auth credentials used for outbound model and API requests.

credentials.description: The new auth credentials to install on the session. When omitted or `undefined`, the call is a no-op and the session's existing credentials are preserved. The runtime stores the value verbatim and uses it for outbound model/API requests; it does NOT re-validate or re-fetch the associated Copilot user response. Several variants carry secret material; treat this method's params as containing secrets at rest and in transit.

params.description: New auth credentials to install on the session. Omit to leave credentials unchanged.
params.title: SessionSetCredentialsParams

result.description: Indicates whether the credential update succeeded.

list.description: Lists canvases declared for the session.

params.description: Identifies the target session.

result.description: Declared canvases available in this session.

listOpen.description: Lists currently open canvas instances for the live session.

params.description: Identifies the target session.

result.description: Live open-canvas snapshot.

open.description: Opens or focuses a canvas instance.

extensionId.description: Owning provider identifier. Optional when the canvasId is unique across providers; required to disambiguate when multiple providers register the same canvasId.

params.description: Canvas open parameters.
params.title: CanvasOpenRequest

result.description: Open canvas instance snapshot.

params.description: Canvas close parameters.
params.title: CanvasCloseRequest

invoke.description: Invokes an action on an open canvas instance.

params.description: Canvas action invocation parameters.
params.title: CanvasActionInvokeRequest

result.description: Canvas action invocation result.

getCurrent.description: Gets the currently selected model for the session.

params.description: Identifies the target session.

result.description: The currently selected model, reasoning effort, and context tier for the session. The context tier reflects `Session.getContextTier()`, restored from the session journal on resume.

switchTo.description: Switches the session to a model and optional reasoning configuration.

reasoningEffort.description: Reasoning effort level to use for the model. "none" disables reasoning.

reasoningSummary.description: Reasoning summary mode to request for supported model clients

modelCapabilities.description: Override individual model capabilities resolved by the runtime

contextTier.description: Explicit context tier for the selected model. `"default"` / `"long_context"` apply the requested tier; omit this field to use normal model behavior with no explicit tier.

params.description: Target model identifier and optional reasoning effort, summary, capability overrides, and context tier.
params.title: ModelSwitchToRequest

result.description: The model identifier active on the session after the switch.

setReasoningEffort.description: Updates the session's reasoning effort without changing the selected model.

reasoningEffort.description: Reasoning effort level to apply to the currently selected model. The host is responsible for validating the value against the model's supported levels before calling.

params.description: Reasoning effort level to apply to the currently selected model.
params.title: ModelSetReasoningEffortRequest

result.description: Update the session's reasoning effort without changing the selected model. Use `switchTo` instead when you also need to change the model. The runtime stores the effort on the session and applies it to subsequent turns.

list.description: Lists models available to this session using its own auth and integration context. Connected hosts (CLI TUI, GitHub App) should call this through the session client so remote sessions return the remote CLI's available models rather than the caller's.

skipCache.description: If true, bypasses the per-session model list cache and re-fetches from CAPI.

params.description: Optional listing options.
params.title: ModelListRequest

result.description: The list of models available to this session.

get.description: Gets the current agent interaction mode.

params.description: Identifies the target session.

result.description: The session mode the agent is operating in

set.description: Sets the current agent interaction mode.

mode.description: The session mode the agent is operating in

params.description: Agent interaction mode to apply to the session.
params.title: ModeSetRequest

params.description: Identifies the target session.

result.description: The session's friendly name, or null when not yet set.

name.description: New session name (1–100 characters, trimmed of leading/trailing whitespace)

params.description: New friendly name to apply to the session.
params.title: NameSetRequest

setAuto.description: Persists an auto-generated session summary as the session's name when no user-set name exists.

summary.description: Auto-generated session summary. Empty/whitespace-only values are ignored; values are trimmed before persisting.

params.description: Auto-generated session summary to apply as the session's name when no user-set name exists.
params.title: NameSetAutoRequest

result.description: Indicates whether the auto-generated summary was applied as the session's name.

read.description: Reads the session plan file from the workspace.

params.description: Identifies the target session.

result.description: Existence, contents, and resolved path of the session plan file.

update.description: Writes new content to the session plan file.

content.description: The new content for the plan file

params.description: Replacement contents to write to the session plan file.
params.title: PlanUpdateRequest

delete.description: Deletes the session plan file from the workspace.

params.description: Identifies the target session.

getWorkspace.description: Gets current workspace metadata for the session.

params.description: Identifies the target session.

result.description: Current workspace metadata for the session, including its absolute filesystem path when available.

listFiles.description: Lists files stored in the session workspace files directory.

params.description: Identifies the target session.

result.description: Relative paths of files stored in the session workspace files directory.

readFile.description: Reads a file from the session workspace files directory.

path.description: Relative path within the workspace files directory

params.description: Relative path of the workspace file to read.
params.title: WorkspacesReadFileRequest

result.description: Contents of the requested workspace file as a UTF-8 string.

createFile.description: Creates or overwrites a file in the session workspace files directory.

path.description: Relative path within the workspace files directory

content.description: File content to write as a UTF-8 string

params.description: Relative path and UTF-8 content for the workspace file to create or overwrite.
params.title: WorkspacesCreateFileRequest

listCheckpoints.description: Lists workspace checkpoints in chronological order.

params.description: Identifies the target session.

result.description: Workspace checkpoints in chronological order; empty when the workspace is not enabled.

readCheckpoint.description: Reads the content of a workspace checkpoint by number.

params.description: Checkpoint number to read.
params.title: WorkspacesReadCheckpointRequest

result.description: Checkpoint content as a UTF-8 string, or null when the checkpoint or workspace is missing.

saveLargePaste.description: Saves pasted content as a UTF-8 file in the session workspace.

content.description: Pasted content to save as a UTF-8 file

params.description: Pasted content to save as a UTF-8 file in the session workspace.
params.title: WorkspacesSaveLargePasteRequest

result.description: Descriptor for the saved paste file, or null when the workspace is unavailable.

diff.description: Computes a diff for the session workspace.

mode.description: Diff mode requested by the client.

params.description: Parameters for computing a workspace diff.
params.title: WorkspacesDiffRequest

result.description: Workspace diff result for the requested mode.

getSources.description: Gets instruction sources loaded for the session.

params.description: Identifies the target session.

result.description: Instruction sources loaded for the session, in merge order.

start.description: Starts fleet mode by submitting the fleet orchestration prompt to the session.

prompt.description: Optional user prompt to combine with fleet instructions

params.description: Optional user prompt to combine with the fleet orchestration instructions.
params.title: FleetStartRequest

result.description: Indicates whether fleet mode was successfully activated.

list.description: Lists custom agents available to the session.

params.description: Identifies the target session.

result.description: Custom agents available to the session.

getCurrent.description: Gets the currently selected custom agent for the session.

params.description: Identifies the target session.

result.description: The currently selected custom agent, or null when using the default agent.

select.description: Selects a custom agent for subsequent turns in the session.

name.description: Name of the custom agent to select

params.description: Name of the custom agent to select for subsequent turns.
params.title: AgentSelectRequest

result.description: The newly selected custom agent.

deselect.description: Clears the selected custom agent and returns the session to the default agent.

params.description: Identifies the target session.

reload.description: Reloads custom agent definitions and returns the refreshed list.

params.description: Identifies the target session.

result.description: Custom agents available to the session after reloading definitions from disk.

startAgent.description: Starts a background agent task in the session.

name.description: Short name for the agent, used to generate a human-readable ID

params.description: Agent type, prompt, name, and optional description and model override for the new task.
params.title: TasksStartAgentRequest

result.description: Identifier assigned to the newly started background agent task.

list.description: Lists background tasks tracked by the session.

params.description: Identifies the target session.

result.description: Background tasks currently tracked by the session.

refresh.description: Refreshes metadata for any detached background shells the runtime knows about.

params.description: Identifies the target session.

result.description: Refresh metadata for any detached background shells the runtime knows about. Use after a long pause to pick up exit/output state for shells running outside the agent loop.

waitForPending.description: Waits for all in-flight background tasks and any follow-up turns to settle.

params.description: Identifies the target session.

result.description: Wait until all in-flight background tasks (agents + shells) and any follow-up turns scheduled by their completions have settled. Returns when the runtime is fully drained or after an internal timeout (default 10 minutes; configurable via COPILOT_TASK_WAIT_TIMEOUT_SECONDS).

getProgress.description: Returns progress information for a background task by ID.

id.description: Task identifier (agent ID or shell ID)

params.description: Identifier of the background task to fetch progress for.
params.title: TasksGetProgressRequest

result.description: Progress information for the task, or null when no task with that ID is tracked.

getCurrentPromotable.description: Returns the first sync-waiting task that can currently be promoted to background mode.

params.description: Identifies the target session.

result.description: The first sync-waiting task that can currently be promoted to background mode.

promoteToBackground.description: Promotes an eligible synchronously-waited task so it continues running in the background.

params.description: Identifier of the task to promote to background mode.
params.title: TasksPromoteToBackgroundRequest

result.description: Indicates whether the task was successfully promoted to background mode.

promoteCurrentToBackground.description: Atomically promotes the first promotable sync-waiting task to background mode and returns it.

params.description: Identifies the target session.

result.description: The promoted task as it now exists in background mode, omitted if no promotable task was waiting.

params.description: Identifier of the background task to cancel.
params.title: TasksCancelRequest

result.description: Indicates whether the background task was successfully cancelled.

remove.description: Removes a completed or cancelled background task from tracking.

params.description: Identifier of the completed or cancelled task to remove from tracking.
params.title: TasksRemoveRequest

result.description: Indicates whether the task was removed. False when the task does not exist or is still running/idle.

sendMessage.description: Sends a message to a background agent task.

message.description: Message content to send to the agent

fromAgentId.description: Agent ID of the sender, if sent on behalf of another agent

params.description: Identifier of the target agent task, message content, and optional sender agent ID.
params.title: TasksSendMessageRequest

result.description: Indicates whether the message was delivered, with an error message when delivery failed.

params.description: Identifies the target session.

result.description: Skills available to the session, with their enabled state.

getInvoked.description: Returns the skills that have been invoked during this session.

params.description: Identifies the target session.

result.description: Skills invoked during this session, ordered by invocation time (most recent last).

name.description: Name of the skill to enable

disable.description: Disables a skill for the session.

params.description: Identifies the target session.

result.description: Diagnostics from reloading skill definitions, with warnings and errors as separate lists.

ensureLoaded.description: Ensures the session's skill definitions have been loaded from disk.

params.description: Identifies the target session.

result.description: MCP servers configured for the session, with their connection status.

enable.description: Enables an MCP server for the session.

serverName.description: Name of the MCP server to enable

params.description: Name of the MCP server to enable for the session.
params.title: McpEnableRequest

disable.description: Disables an MCP server for the session.

serverName.description: Name of the MCP server to disable

params.description: Name of the MCP server to disable for the session.

reload.description: Reloads MCP server connections for the session.

params.description: Identifies the target session.

executeSampling.description: Runs an MCP sampling inference on behalf of an MCP server.

requestId.description: Caller-provided unique identifier for this sampling execution. Use this same ID with cancelSamplingExecution to cancel the in-flight call. Must be unique within the session for the lifetime of the call.

serverName.description: Name of the MCP server that initiated the sampling request

mcpRequestId.description: The original MCP JSON-RPC request ID (string or number). Used by the runtime to correlate the inference with the originating MCP request for telemetry; this is distinct from `requestId` (which is the schema-level cancellation handle).

request.description: Raw MCP CreateMessageRequest params, as received in the `sampling.requested` event. Treated as opaque at the schema layer; the runtime converts the embedded MCP messages into the OpenAI chat-completion shape internally.

params.description: Identifiers and raw MCP CreateMessageRequest params used to run a sampling inference.
params.title: McpExecuteSamplingParams

result.description: Outcome of an MCP sampling execution: success result, failure error, or cancellation.

cancelSamplingExecution.description: Cancels an in-flight MCP sampling execution by request ID.

requestId.description: The requestId previously passed to executeSampling that should be cancelled

params.description: The requestId previously passed to executeSampling that should be cancelled.
params.title: McpCancelSamplingExecutionParams

result.description: Indicates whether an in-flight sampling execution with the given requestId was found and cancelled.

setEnvValueMode.description: Sets how environment-variable values supplied to MCP servers are resolved (direct or indirect).

mode.description: How environment-variable values supplied to MCP servers are resolved. "direct" passes literal string values; "indirect" treats values as references (e.g. names of environment variables on the host) that the runtime resolves before launch. Defaults to the runtime's startup mode; clients that intentionally launch MCP servers with literal values (e.g. CLI prompt mode and ACP) set this to "direct".

params.description: Mode controlling how MCP server env values are resolved (`direct` or `indirect`).
params.title: McpSetEnvValueModeParams

result.description: Env-value mode recorded on the session after the update.

removeGitHub.description: Removes the auto-managed `github` MCP server when present.

params.description: Identifies the target session.

result.description: Indicates whether the auto-managed `github` MCP server was removed (false when nothing to remove).

login.description: Starts OAuth authentication for a remote MCP server.

serverName.description: Name of the remote MCP server to authenticate

forceReauth.description: When true, clears any cached OAuth token for the server and runs a full new authorization. Use when the user explicitly wants to switch accounts or believes their session is stuck.

clientName.description: Optional override for the OAuth client display name shown on the consent screen. Applies to newly registered dynamic clients only — existing registrations keep the name they were created with. When omitted, the runtime applies a neutral fallback; callers driving interactive auth should pass their own surface-specific label so the consent screen matches the product the user sees.

callbackSuccessMessage.description: Optional override for the body text shown on the OAuth loopback callback success page. When omitted, the runtime applies a neutral fallback; callers driving interactive auth should pass surface-specific copy telling the user where to return.

params.description: Remote MCP server name and optional overrides controlling reauthentication, OAuth client display name, and the callback success-page copy.
params.title: McpOauthLoginRequest

result.description: OAuth authorization URL the caller should open, or empty when cached tokens already authenticated the server.

readResource.description: Fetch an MCP resource (typically a `ui://` MCP App bundle, per SEP-1865) from a connected server. Requires the `mcp-apps` session capability.

serverName.description: Name of the MCP server hosting the resource

params.description: MCP server and resource URI to fetch.
params.title: McpAppsReadResourceRequest

result.description: Resource contents returned by the MCP server.

listTools.description: List tools that an MCP App view is allowed to call (SEP-1865 visibility filter). Returns tools whose `_meta.ui.visibility` is unset (default `["model","app"]`) or includes `"app"`.

originServerName.description: **Required.** Server whose ui:// view issued the request. Per SEP-1865 ('callable by the app from this server only'), the call is rejected when this differs from `serverName`, and rejected outright when missing.

params.description: MCP server to list app-callable tools for.
params.title: McpAppsListToolsRequest

result.description: App-callable tools from the named MCP server.

callTool.description: Call an MCP tool from an MCP App view (SEP-1865). Enforces the visibility check that prevents an app iframe from invoking model-only tools. Returns the standard MCP `CallToolResult`.

originServerName.description: **Required.** Server whose ui:// view issued the request. Per SEP-1865 ('callable by the app from this server only'), the call is rejected when this differs from `serverName`, and rejected outright when missing.

params.description: MCP server, tool name, and arguments to invoke from an MCP App view.
params.title: McpAppsCallToolRequest

result.description: Standard MCP CallToolResult

setHostContext.description: Replace the host context returned to MCP App guests on `ui/initialize`. Hosts use this to advertise theme, locale, or other metadata to the guest UI.

context.description: Host context advertised to MCP App guests

params.description: Host context to advertise to MCP App guests.
params.title: McpAppsSetHostContextRequest

getHostContext.description: Read the current host context advertised to MCP App guests.

params.description: Identifies the target session.

result.description: Current host context advertised to MCP App guests.

diagnose.description: Diagnose MCP Apps wiring for a specific MCP server. Reports the session capability, feature-flag state, advertised extension, and how many tools have `_meta.ui` populated.

params.description: MCP server to diagnose MCP Apps wiring for.
params.title: McpAppsDiagnoseRequest

result.description: Diagnostic snapshot of MCP Apps wiring for the named server.

list.description: Lists plugins installed for the session.

suppressCustomAgentPrompt.description: When true, the selected custom agent's prompt is not injected into the user message (skill context is still injected). Used by automation triggers where the agent prompt is already in the problem statement.

disabledInstructionSources.description: Instruction source IDs to exclude from the system prompt.

coauthorEnabled.description: Whether to include the `Co-authored-by` trailer in commit messages.

enableReasoningSummaries.description: Whether to surface reasoning-summary events from the model.

eventsLogDirectory.description: Override directory for the session-events log. When unset, the runtime's default events log directory is used.

additionalContentExclusionPolicies.description: Additional content-exclusion policies to merge into the session's policy set. Opaque shape; see `ContentExclusionApiResponse` in the runtime.

manageScheduleEnabled.description: Whether to expose the `manage_schedule` tool to the agent. The runtime always owns the per-session schedule registry; this flag only controls tool exposure (typically gated to staff users).

skipEmbeddingRetrieval.description: Whether to skip embedding retrieval pipeline initialization and execution.

organizationCustomInstructions.description: Organization-level custom instructions to inject into the system prompt.

enableFileHooks.description: Whether to enable loading of `.github/hooks/` filesystem hooks. Separate from the SDK callback hook mechanism.

enableSessionStore.description: Whether to enable cross-session store writes and reads.

enableSkills.description: Whether to enable skill directory scanning and loading. Falls back to enableConfigDiscovery when unset.

registerInterest.description: Registers consumer interest in an event type for runtime gating purposes.

eventType.description: The event type the consumer wants the runtime to treat as 'observed' for behavior-switching gating. Some runtime code paths inspect whether any consumer is interested in a specific event type and choose a different implementation accordingly (e.g. `mcp.oauth_required`: when interest is registered the runtime delegates the full interactive OAuth flow to the consumer; when no interest is registered the runtime installs a browserless fallback that silently reuses cached tokens). SDK clients that long-poll events do NOT automatically appear as listeners to these gating checks — they must explicitly call `registerInterest` for each event type they want the runtime to count as having a consumer. Multiple registrations for the same event type from the same or different consumers are tracked independently and must each be released. See: `mcp.oauth_required`, `sampling.requested`, `auto_mode_switch.requested`, `user_input.requested`, `elicitation.requested`, `command.queued`, `exit_plan_mode.requested`.

waitMs.description: Milliseconds to wait for new events when the cursor is at the tail of history. 0 (default) returns immediately even if no events are available. Capped at 30000ms. Ephemeral events that arrive during the wait are delivered in this batch but are NOT replayable on a subsequent read (use a non-zero waitMs in your next call to capture future ephemerals as they happen).

eventType.description: The event type the consumer wants the runtime to treat as 'observed' for behavior-switching gating. Some runtime code paths inspect whether any consumer is interested in a specific event type and choose a different implementation accordingly (e.g. `mcp.oauth_required`: when interest is registered the runtime delegates the full interactive OAuth flow to the consumer; when no interest is registered the runtime installs a browserless fallback that silently reuses cached tokens). SDK clients that long-poll events do NOT automatically appear as listeners to these gating checks — they must explicitly call `registerInterest` for each event type they want the runtime to count as having a consumer. Multiple registrations for the same event type from the same or different consumers are tracked independently and must each be released. See: `mcp.oauth_required`, `sampling.requested`, `auto_mode_switch.requested`, `user_input.requested`, `elicitation.requested`, `command.queued`, `exit_plan_mode.requested`.

RegisterEventInterestParams.description: Event type to register consumer interest for, used by runtime gating logic.
RegisterEventInterestParams.title: RegisterEventInterestParams

handle.description: Opaque handle for this registration. Pass to releaseInterest to release. Each call to registerInterest produces a fresh handle, even when the same eventType is registered multiple times.

RegisterEventInterestResult.description: Opaque handle representing an event-type interest registration.
RegisterEventInterestResult.title: RegisterEventInterestResult

handle.description: Handle returned by a previous `registerInterest` call. Idempotent: releasing an unknown or already-released handle is a no-op (returns success). When the last outstanding handle for an event type is released, the runtime reverts to its 'no consumer' code path for that event type.

ReleaseEventInterestParams.description: Opaque handle previously returned by `registerInterest` to release.
ReleaseEventInterestParams.title: ReleaseEventInterestParams

Multiple registrations are reference-counted; the server bridge will only dispatch auto-mode-switch requests when no handles are active.

UIRegisterDirectAutoModeSwitchHandlerRes.description: Register an in-process handler for `auto_mode_switch.requested` events. The caller still attaches the actual listener via the standard event-subscription mechanism; this registration solely tells the server bridge to skip its own dispatch (so a remote client doesn't race the in-process handler for the same requestId).
